# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.

Prevent SQL injection.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php
// Assuming you have a PDO connection $pdo

function generateReport() {
    global $pdo;

    // Initialize filter variables
    $status = isset($_POST['status']) ? $_POST['status'] : null;
    $category = isset($_POST['category']) ? $_POST['category'] : null;
    $startDate = isset($_POST['start_date']) ? $_POST['start_date'] : null;
    $endDate = isset($_POST['end_date']) ? $_POST['end_date'] : null;

    // Start building the SQL query
    $sql = "SELECT * FROM reports WHERE 1=1";

    // Add conditions based on filters
    if ($status) {
        $sql .= " AND status = :status";
    }
    if ($category) {
        $sql .= " AND category = :category";
    }
    if ($startDate && $endDate) {
        $sql .= " AND date BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date";
    }

    // Prepare the statement
    $stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql);

    // Bind parameters
    if ($status) {
        $stmt->bindParam(':status', $status);
    }
    if ($category) {
        $stmt->bindParam(':category', $category);
    }
    if ($startDate && $endDate) {
        $stmt->bindParam(':start_date', $startDate);
        $stmt->bindParam(':end_date', $endDate);
    }

    // Execute the query
    $stmt->execute();

    // Fetch and return results
    $results = $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);

    return $results;
}

// Example usage
$reportData = generateReport();
print_r($reportData);
?>
